welcomeMessage.en: Today we are getting ready to explore the world as we talk all about travel!
welcomeMessage.zh: 今天我们要一起探索世界，聊一聊关于旅行的话题！

welcomeMessage.en: Traveling is one of those subjects everyone loves talking about because it is full of amazing memories, and sometimes, funny disasters.
welcomeMessage.zh: 旅行是每个人都喜欢谈论的话题之一，因为它充满了美好的回忆，有时还有有趣的意外和灾难。

welcomeMessage.en: We will share our dream destinations, the best trips we've ever taken, and the things that drive us crazy when we travel.
welcomeMessage.zh: 我们将分享我们梦想中的目的地，我们经历过的最棒的旅行，以及旅行中让我们抓狂的事情。

questionsToThinkAbout.en: What is the most beautiful or memorable place you have ever visited?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 你去过的最美丽或最难忘的地方是哪里？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: If money and time were not an issue, where would you travel to right now?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 如果金钱和时间都不是问题，你现在最想去哪里旅行？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: Do you prefer an active vacation (like hiking and exploring) or a relaxing vacation (like lying on a beach)?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 你更喜欢充满活力地度假（比如远足和探索），还是放松的度假（比如躺在海滩上）？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: Have you ever had a terrible or funny travel disaster? What happened?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 你有没有经历过可怕或有趣的旅行灾难？发生了什么？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: When you travel, what is the one thing you absolutely must pack in your suitcase?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 旅行时，什么是你行李箱里绝对不可缺少的东西？

vocabulary.definition: The place to which someone or something is going or being sent.
vocabulary.example: "Paris is a very popular tourist destination."

vocabulary.definition: A planned route or journey; a schedule of activities.
vocabulary.example: "Our travel itinerary is packed with museum visits."

vocabulary.definition: The activity of visiting places of interest in a particular location.
vocabulary.example: "We spent the whole day sightseeing in London."

vocabulary.definition: A thing that is kept as a reminder of a person, place, or event.
vocabulary.example: "I bought this keychain as a souvenir from my trip."

vocabulary.definition: Suitcases or other bags in which to pack personal belongings for traveling.
vocabulary.example: "We waited for an hour to get our luggage at the airport."

vocabulary.definition: To walk or move in a leisurely, casual, or aimless way.
vocabulary.example: "I love to just wander around a new city and get lost."

vocabulary.definition: Drained of one's physical or mental resources; very tired.
vocabulary.example: "After a twelve-hour flight, we were completely exhausted."

vocabulary.definition: Astonishing or awe-inspiring in quality, so as to take one's breath away.
vocabulary.translation: 激动人心的 / 美得令人惊叹的
vocabulary.example: "The view from the top of the mountain was absolutely breathtaking."

vocabulary.definition: The feeling of disorientation experienced by someone suddenly subjected to an unfamiliar culture.
vocabulary.example: "When I first moved to a new country, I experienced a lot of culture shock."

vocabulary.definition: An inhabitant of a particular area or neighborhood.
vocabulary.example: "We decided to ask a local for restaurant recommendations."

meaning.en: To set out on a journey or leave.
meaning.zh: 启程开始一段旅程或离开。

commonPhrases.example: "It's getting late, we should probably hit the road."

meaning.en: To travel with a minimum of luggage.

commonPhrases.example: "I always travel light so I don't have to carry a heavy suitcase everywhere."

commonPhrases.translation: 人迹罕至的地方 / 冷门景点

meaning.en: A place where few people go, far from any main roads and towns.
meaning.zh: 很少有人去的地方，远离主要道路和城镇；冷门景点。

commonPhrases.example: "We found a beautiful restaurant completely off the beaten path."

goals: Encourage storytelling: Keep them talking about the details of their favorite or worst trips. If they say 'it was good', ask 'Who did you go with?' or 'What did you eat there?'
Grammar focus: Listen closely to their past tense verbs as they describe past vacations.

phases.title: Phase 1: Warm-Up Questions (10 Minutes)
phases.description: General questions to enter the topic easily.
questions: Do you prefer traveling by plane, train, or car?
Are you someone who likes to plan an exact itinerary, or do you prefer to be spontaneous?
Do you usually buy souvenirs when you travel?

phases.title: Phase 2: Deep Dive (30 Minutes)
questions: What is the most beautiful destination you've ever seen with your own eyes?
Tell me a story about a time when something went totally wrong on a trip (e.g., getting lost, missing a flight, bad weather).
What is the absolute best food you've ever eaten while traveling?
Have you ever experienced 'culture shock'? What was it like?

phases.title: Phase 3: Fun Roleplay (10 Minutes)
questions: Roleplay Challenge: Imagine we are at the airport, and I am the rude worker checking your bags. Your bag is 5 kilograms overweight, but you absolutely refuse to pay the extra fee. Convince me to let you go!
